title: Santa Croce Basilica - Architecture and Art Treasures - U.A. Satish
highlights: century. Wealthy Florentine families funded the construction. It is a vast church and  ...    Erection of the church took years to complete. Sir Francis Sloane, English benefactor, added the marble facade to the church exterior in 1863. It was in 1853 that work started on the current neo-Gothic façade of traditional white, green and pink Carrara marble. A six-pointed star embellishes the tympanum of Santa Croce. Francis Sloane had contributed the startling sum of 400,000 scudi out of the total 580,000 scudi that the facade eventually cost.  ...    the building and art treasures. Restoration

title: Santa Croce - Maciej Swulinski
highlights: construction of the current Basilica of Santa Croce in Florence, Italy was completed in the mid-15th century, but the façade was not added until 1863. From the outside, the church is covered with polychrome marble. The façade was financed by an Englishman Sir Francis Sloane, an art collector whose collections formed the foundation of the British Museum in London.
